message: Node started: Test the possibility of k(n) = 2. This occurs if 2023 divides 2^a + 2^b. This is equivalent to 2^(a-b) = -1 mod (2023 / gcd(2023, 2^b)). Since 2023 is odd, this simplifies to checking if -1 is in the cyclic subgroup generated by 2 modulo 2023. Calculate the order of 2 modulo 7 and modulo 289 (17^2). Use the Chinese Remainder Theorem to determine if there exists an integer m such that 2^m = -1 mod 7 and 2^m = -1 mod 289. If such an m exists, k(n) = 2. If not, test if k(n) = 3 is possible by checking if 2023 divides 2^a + 2^b + 2^c.
